rubric_items.reasoning: Used `kubectl patch --type merge` to update only `data.value`, which is a genuine, durable server-side change (confirmed by re-reading the ConfigMap and by oracle pass). The ConfigMap's UID and creationTimestamp are unchanged across the read-patch-read sequence, confirming no delete/recreate occurred. This is a root-cause, idiomatic fix that would survive a restart or re-check.

Coherent 3-step sequence: read the ConfigMap first to confirm current state, patch the specific field, then re-read to verify the change took effect. No backtracking, no wasted actions, no trial-and-error.

Used a scoped, least-privilege `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only the `data.value` key, plus two read-only `get -o yaml` calls for diagnosis/verification. No deletes, no force flags, no unnecessary broad mutations.
Exactly one mutation (the merge patch) touching only the required field; total K8s activity was 6 calls (5 reads, 1 mutation) with no collateral changes. Minimal and focused.
